rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN ROBINSON, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO VS. DOE 1
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 25, 2026, Line 1. 2 - Defendant Thaddeus Doyka's Motion to Quash Service of Process is GRANTED. Plaintiff has the evidentiary burden to demonstrate proper service and sufficient minimum contacts to warrant this court's exercise of jurisdiction. (Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1440 ["[T]he burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service"].) Plaintiff Jonathan Robinson maintains that he substitute served Doyka on July 13, 2025. Substitute service is authorized if personal service cannot be accomplished with reasonable diligence. (CCP section 415.20(b).) First, upon challenge, plaintiff must demonstrate he attempted personal service with "reasonable diligence." (CCP section 415.20(b); see Evartt v. Superior Court (1979) 89 Cal.App.3d 795, 801.) Two or three attempts at personal service ordinarily suffice. (Stafford v. Mach (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1174, 1182.) Second, plaintiff must establish substitute service by demonstrating he left the papers with a competent adult apparently in charge at the person's homes, usual place of business or usual mailing address other than a USPS box. (CCP section 415.20(b).) Plaintiff's purported service is defective. First, the process server fails to show personal service attempts. (Simons Decl., Ex. 9 [Castillo Decl.].) Second, plaintiff does not demonstrate that the Rancho Mirage address is defendant's "usual place of business" within the meaning of CCP 415.20(b). At most, plaintiff has shown that (1) defendant was less than truthful when he previously stated that "I do not conduct any business at that address" and (2) he is affiliated with Gray Holdings, Inc., which lists the Rancho Mirage address. (Simons Decl., Ex. 3, 1:27-28; Ex. 6.) Notwithstanding defendant's lack of credibility, plaintiff still does not demonstrate that the address is defendant's "usual place of business." For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN ROBINSON, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO VS. DOE 1
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss First-Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 25, 2026, Line 1. 1 - Defendant Thaddeus Doyka's motion to dismiss is DENIED. A plaintiff must serve a defendant within three years of commencing the action. (CCP 583.210(a).) The failure to do so shall lead to a dismissal. (CCP 583.250.) Under CCP 583.240, however, the time is excluded where the defendant is not amenable to the process of the court (CCP 583.240(a)); the prosecution of the action was stayed (CCP 583.240(b)); the validity of service was subject of litigation (CCP 583.240(c)); and when service is "impossible, impracticable, or futile." (583.240(d)). Here, under CCP 340.11(i), the time for service commenced on 12/21/22 when the court issued its order regarding the certificate of merit and reasonable cause to file this action. Plaintiff argues that the time to serve was tolled for 172 days while the parties litigated several motions regarding service. The court agrees that there is tolling but under CCP 583.240(c), calculates the time differently. The court calculated the days based on when the motions to quash were filed and the orders resolving them were entered. The court also added time for the instant motion to quash. 2/29/24 - 6/3/24 (95 days) 7/18/24 - 8/16/24 (29 days) 12/30/24 - 3/10/26 (435 days) 5/13/26 - 6/25/26 (43 days) Based on the above (602 days), the time to serve has yet to run. The three-year period based on the current record will run on August 14, 2027. The court further notes that this case was in default from 12/6/23 to 5/31/24. But that time is not excluded. (Dale v. ITT Life Ins. Corp. (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 495 [erroneous entry of default judgment against the defendant did not toll the period for service of process]; Shipley v. Sugita (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 320, 326 [the exclusions under CCP section 583.240 "must be construed strictly against the plaintiff."].) Plaintiff must exercise diligence, service is usually within the control of the plaintiff, and plaintiff does not show that impracticability of service was due to causes beyond plaintiff's control. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: NOZIPHO BECKER VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Action Pursuant To Ccp 377.31 And Substitute Plaintiff Nozipho Becker, Ph.D., Deceased, With Personal Representative Gabriel Becker
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 25, 2026, Line 5. Plaintiff Nozipho Becker, PhD's Motion To Continue Action Pursuant To CCP 377.31 And Substitute Plaintiff Nozipho Becker, Ph.D., Deceased, With Personal Representative Gabriel Becker is GRANTED. Plaintiff moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 377.31: "On motion after the death of a person who commenced an action or proceeding, the court shall allow a pending action or proceeding that does not abate to be continued by the decedent's personal representative or, if none, by the decedent's successor in interest." The motion must be accompanied by certain information, including a sworn declaration under section 377.31. Good cause appearing, an appropriate showing under Code of Civil Procedure sections 377.32 having been made and the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure sections 377.31 having been met, the court grants the motion. The court orders that the action may continue and Gabriel Becker, decedent Nozipho Becker, PhD's personal representative, shall be substituted in as plaintiff. Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of this tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. rulings.Case Title: AHMED ALSHERIF ET AL VS. YUJIN EMILY KIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Submit A Tardy Expert Designation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Matt Burgess, Esq. And Mackenzie Lusby; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 25, 2026, Line 7. Plaintiffs Ahmed Alsherif and Abdi Jamal Ismail's unopposed Motion for Leave to Submit a Tardy Expert Designation is GRANTED. Plaintiffs move under Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.720. The court shall grant leave to submit tardy expert witness information only after the court considers three major factors and makes corresponding findings. First, the court must consider "the extent to which the opposing party has relied on the absence of a list of expert witnesses." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.720(a).) Second, the court must find "that any party opposing the motion will not be prejudiced in maintaining that party's action or defense on the merits." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.720(b).) Third, the court must find "the moving party did all of the following: (1) [f]ailed to submit the information as the result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect[;] (2) [s]ought leave to submit the information promptly after learning of the mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect[; and] (3) [p]romptly thereafter served a copy of the proposed expert witness information described in Section 2034.260 on all other parties who have appeared in the action. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.720(b).) The conditions set forth in section 2034.720 having been satisfied and good cause appearing, the court grants Plaintiffs leave to submit their tardy expert designations, which, in fact, were served electronically on May 27, 2026. This order is conditioned on Plaintiffs making the experts available immediately for a deposition under Article 3 (commencing with Section 2034.410). Concerns regarding the trial date must be addressed in the master calendar department. Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of this tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, LLC VS. EUROPEAN COLLISION CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 25, 2026, Line 11. DEFENDANT EUROPEAN COLLISION CENTER, LLC DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The parties failed to meet and confer in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41. The code requires a meet and confer in person, by phone, or video conference. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.41, subd. (a).) The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Notably, this is the second time a demurrer must be taken off calendar for this reason. The response to the complaint is due in 15 days, 15 days running from notice of this order. Defendant shall lodge a proposed order containing the above text verbatim by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
